clubs.title: Torpedo Masters Club
clubs.description:
The Torpedo Masters Club is a dynamic swim program designed for masters swimmers of all skill levels from fitness enthusiasts and former competitive athletes to newcomers discovering the sport later in life. Our mission is to foster a lifelong love for swimming while building strength, endurance, and community through structured training and support.

Our coach is USMS athlete and USAS coach Alex Bezugliy provides personalized workouts focused on technique refinement, aerobic conditioning, and goal-oriented progress, whether members are training for competitions, triathlons, or personal fitness. The team atmosphere is both motivating and welcoming, offering camaraderie, accountability, and a shared passion for excellence in the water.

With access to top-tier facility in Port Charlotte, FL, our club empowers adults to push their limits, improve their swim skills, and stay active in a fun, challenging, and encouraging environment.
clubs.practiceTimes: Monday, Wednesday, Friday 10.30 am -12.30 pm year round at Centennial Pool in Port Charlotte, FL

clubs.title: Sarasota Tsunami Masters
clubs.description:
Sarasota Tsunami Masters caters to all levels of adult swimmers, from beginners in our adult learn-to-swim classes, to fitness swimmers, to those who compete in swim meets, triathlons, and open-water swims. Our USMS-certified coaches and instructors have the expertise and desire to help you reach your swimming goals, whatever they may be.
We offer organized and challenging workouts, as well as a friendly and welcoming team culture, in up to nine workouts a week.
We also are a USMS Swimming Saves Lives Foundation adult-learn-to-swim grant program. Ask us how we can help you learn to swim and be safer and happier in the water.

clubs.title: Swim Florida Masters
clubs.description:
The health benefits of swimming are indisputable and Swim Florida has been developing champion age group swimmers for over 45 years. 
Swim Florida Masters recognizes these benefits and we provide our adult swimmers (18 & older) with structured training in a fun, social and competitive environment for all levels.
Our swimmers range in ability from beginners who are just getting into the sport of swimming and are looking to improve their health to advanced level athletes who have completed Ironman races, swam the English Channel and so much more!

clubs.title: T2 Naples Masters
clubs.description:
T2 Masters is for adults ages 18 and up!
At all practices, all levels of swimming are welcome. We have all ability levels from swimmers that are new to the sport, to fitness swimmers, to competitive triathlete and masters swimmers. 
At T2 we are interested in helping you achieve your goals, whatever they may be. The coaching staff wants to take the time to help you learn how to swim with proper technique to prevent injury and increase speed and endurance.
We offer several coached practices a week in the morning and afternoon. There are athletes with many different goals and ability levels. Our staff can help you raise the level of your performance no matter where you are starting.

clubs.title: St Pete Masters Inc
clubs.description: The St. Pete Masters home facility is the North Shore Aquatic Complex located in beautiful downtown St. Petersburg. Boasting a 50 meter x 25 yard pool and a 25 meter x 25 yard pool with stunning views of Tampa Bay, the complex is open year-round with both pools being heated and cooled to an average temperature of 81-82 degrees. St. Pete Masters has 11 coached workouts available each week, but registered USMS members may use the pool facility anytime it is open to the public as well as the assigned practice times.
clubs.practiceTimes: Please see website for practice schedule (https://stpetemasters.org).  You don't need to contact us before you arrive at a practice, but give yourself enough time to check in and pay the city fee of $7 at the front desk and then show your USMS member card to the coach on deck and pay the $5 drop-in fee.  Thank you! We look forward to seeing you.

clubs.title: Clearwater Aquatic Masters
clubs.description:
CAM is a top-notch maters swim program based in Pinellas County let by 3-time Olympic Gold Medalist Brooke Bennett and her coaching staff. All coaches are USMS
certified, Red Cross Safety Training for Swim Coaches, and CPR/First Aid/AED Certified. KLR/CAM offers 10 workouts weekly, offering long-course meters, short course yards, and open-water.
Practices are ALWAYS coached and offer four different swim groups (based on short-course yards intervals): Bolts: 1:30 and under; Rays: 1:40 and under; Bucs: 1:50 and under; Rowdies: 2:00 and up. Each swim workout is written to challenge with a mix of distance, middle distance/stroke, and for your need-for-speed we got that covered too. CAM has something to offer everyone, because we all share the passion to be happy, healthy, and fit no matter what level we are striving for.
In addition to the 10 scheduled workouts KLR/CAM will offer stroke technique and open-water clinics throughout the year. A calendar of event listings will be provided
for swim meets, open-water swims, and triathlons locally and across the globe. It is encouraged for each swimmer to have a swim bag with kickboard, fins, pull buoy, paddles, and snorkel (optional). Prerequisite able to swim 200 yards free continuously. 
KLR/CAM monthly dues are $70 and $65 a month for Kennedy Law Racing Members. The daily drop-in fee is $10 cash or check on deck the day of the swim.
 “Catch the Green Wave” with KLR/CAM Program TODAY!

clubs.title: Swim Out
clubs.description:
Central Florida's premier, all-inclusive, masters swimming and social club! We welcome all individuals (age 18+) regardless of sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, race or ability to this organization designed to foster fun, friendship, fitness, and health through sport, competition and social events. This club is a member of United States Masters Swimming and promotes the life-long activity of swimming.

This group establishes a friendly masters team that brings together adults with the love of swimming for fitness, camaraderie, competition and friendship on a regular basis.

The focus is both social and fitness, with the team being an official USMS club, having regular swim workouts, and some relaxed meetups occasionally after swims for lunch, coffee, etc.

If you have interest in getting together to meet others who share a genuine interest in swimming and other social events, please join the group! The website is www.swimout.org.

clubs.title: Swim Start
clubs.description:
At Swim Start, we believe that swimming is more than just a skill; it’s a path to fitness, recreation, personal growth, and friendships. Our program is designed for all fitness levels who want to learn to swim, improve their technique, or enhance overall swimming fitness. Our USMS certified instructors are experts in providing personalized one-on-one and small group lessons. Whether you’re a complete beginner, a former swimmer returning to sport, or an intermediate swimmer looking to refine your strokes and increase endurance, we can customize a program to meet your needs. We provide a variety of skills and support to assist you in reaching your goals:
•	Overcoming fear of water and building confidence
•	Breathing, sculling, and treading water
•	Recreational swimming
•	Learning the four competitive strokes
•	Preparing to join a master’s swim team, workout group, or pass a swim test
Contact Coach Gina to set up an evaluation and personalized swim fitness plan.
clubs.practiceTimes:
Private lessons by appointment and group workouts.
See available classes, packages, and membership options: https://foractive.app.link/pro/swim-start-llc

clubs.title: Lake Highland Masters Swimming Club
clubs.description:
* On deck coach with daily workout plans. 
* Heated and air-rated, 8 lane 50 meter pool, 24 lane 25 yard 
course. 
* Men and Women's locker rooms with showers. 
* Conducted at the Lake Highland Preparatory School's pool. 
* USMS Membership is required to attend.

Workout times: 
We have two groups to choose from:

The "90 Minute Group" swims Monday, Wednesday, Friday: 5:30am - 7:00am. 
* The cost is $50 monthly or a $10 drop in

The "45 Minute Group" swims Monday, Wednesday, Friday: 5:30 am - 7:00 am or 6:15am - 7:00am. 
* The cost is $30 monthly or a $5 drop 
* This shorter program is great for: 
- Those starting masters swimming. 
- Building endurance, strength / fitness and improving stroke technique. 
- Those who are already familiar with masters swimming but want a shorter workout. 
- Lap Swimmers 
- Those who want to sleep in a little a later
https://www.facebook.com/LHMSC/
Contact: LHMSwimming@gmail.com

clubs.title: City Of Miramar
clubs.description:
The City of Miramar Masters Swimming Club is a professionally organized aquatic program dedicated to promoting health, fitness, and competitive excellence among adult swimmers. Our club provides a structured training environment designed for swimmers of all experience levels, from individuals seeking to improve their fitness and swimming technique to experienced athletes pursuing competitive Masters swimming.

Our program focuses on stroke development, endurance training, and overall aquatic conditioning while fostering a supportive and inclusive team atmosphere. Through professionally guided workouts, technical instruction, and organized training sessions, we aim to help members achieve their personal fitness and performance goals.

The City of Miramar Masters Swimming Club is committed to promoting lifelong participation in swimming, encouraging community engagement, and supporting the principles of sportsmanship, safety, and aquatic education. As part of the Masters swimming community, we strive to provide opportunities for recreational, fitness, and competitive swimming in a positive and motivating environment.
clubs.practiceTimes:
Training sessions are held Monday through Friday from 6:00 AM to 7:30 AM, providing consistent opportunities for members to train, improve their skills, and maintain an active lifestyle.

The City of Miramar Masters Swimming Club is committed to promoting lifelong participation in swimming, encouraging community engagement, and supporting the principles of sportsmanship, safety, and aquatic education.

clubs.title: Doral Swim Club
clubs.description:
Welcome to Doral Swim Club

We are excited to welcome you and your swimmer(s) to Doral Swim Club. Our program is a year-round competitive swimming organization offering high-quality instruction, training, and competition opportunities for athletes ages 6 to 18, as well as a Masters Swimming program for adults. Doral Swim Club operates at the Doral Aquatic Center in partnership with the City of Doral Parks & Recreation Department.

Doral Swim Club is committed to providing a positive, educational, and athlete-centered environment. Our coaching staff follows all USA Swimming and U.S. Center for SafeSport policies to ensure that every participant trains and competes in a safe, respectful, and supportive setting.

We believe in building champions in and out of the pool. Our mission is to nurture discipline, sportsmanship and personal growth while positioning Doral as a hub of aquatic excellence for athletes of all ages.

We look forward to guiding your swimmer’s development and helping them achieve their full potential both in and out of the water.

clubs.title: North Florida Swimming
clubs.description: Following the philosophy and teachings of North Florida Swimming, and the legendary Coach Mel Nash, we focus on stroke technique as the primary means of increasing speed in the water and preventing injury. This club is headed by Coach Katie Chiang, who has been swimming for 16+ years (13 of those under Coach Mel) and coaching and teaching for 7+ years (with a primary focus on stroke analysis and technique). As a pre-med student with a Bachelor's in biomedical sciences and 5+ years of experience working in the medical field, Coach Katie has a solid foundation and understanding of the human body, its mechanics, and the effects of various health issues on performance (and how swimming can alleviate many of these problems and improve health and fitness). All skill levels welcome, as we tailor to each individual, creating personalized plans based on age, swimming experience, medical history, and fitness or competition goals.

clubs.title: Palmetto Masters
clubs.description:
The Palmetto Masters Swim Team is the largest USMS team in South Carolina and is open to those 18 years of age and older interested in fitness and/or competitive swimming. All levels and abilities are welcome.

We have various training locations to choose from based on your residence and preference. More than 25 deck coached practices are available each week! Competitions are available through USMS (United States Masters Swimming). Participation is encouraged but not required (meets, open water swims, USMS fitness events, triathlons).
clubs.practiceTimes: Visit the club website for workout info

clubs.title: Eastern Shore Masters Swimming
clubs.description: We live the 'freestyle lifestyle'! Train for fitness, fun or competition. Our swimmers have varied skill levels and come from different backgrounds. We welcome former HS & collegiate swimmers, triathletes and those who just want to stay fit. We love to share a lane with swimmers of all ages 18+ .
clubs.practiceTimes:
MWF @5:30am - 6:45am; Sat @8:00am - 9:30am. 
What will the workouts be like? The workouts are currently team-led using workouts from USA Swimming certified coaches, and in the future the club will be coached/instructed by a USA Swimming certified swim coach.  The workouts will cater to adults wishing to work on overall swimming fitness, compete in adult competitions, and train for triathlons.  Workouts will consist of a mix of cardiovascular training and stroke technique.  Competition is not required, only promoted.  If you are interested in competing in adult swim meets, the team is registered with United States Masters Swimming. Whether you can stay the entire workout or not, we encourage you to come even if it's just 30 minutes of swim time!  All swimmers are welcome, from all swim levels and backgrounds!!!


I’m not a competitive swimmer, can I still join?  Yes!  We will run different workouts for different levels. Swimmers are welcome to take everything at their own pace and do what they can. Everyone must be water safe and able to swim multiple lengths of the pool unassisted.

clubs.title: Auburn Master Swimmers
clubs.description: Great coaches, great team mates, students, faculty, townies, wide range of talents all focused on fun and fitness.  We swim in one of the best competition pools in the U.S. (Auburn University) and also spend much of the year in a lovely outdoor 50 meter pool, weather permitting!

clubs.title: City of Mobile Masters
clubs.description:
Welcome to CMSA Masters! City of Mobile Masters (CMSA) is a team open to anyone over the age of 18 and to varying levels of ability.  Our group is not intended for those who need beginning swimming lessons or who are not physically well enough to swim. Those interested in swimming with our team must be considered water safe and be able to complete 25-yard laps without assistance over the course of an hour. Our team is made up of students, staff, and community members spanning several decades and a wide range of abilities.  As a swim club, we have regularly scheduled workouts with qualified swim coaches who work with each swimmer to improve technique, overall fitness, and speed. CMSA Masters is a team registered with United States Masters Swimming.  All CMSA Masters members must belong to USMS, which provides liability insurance coverage to individuals, the club, and Bishop State Community College. 
We hope to catch you at a workout!

clubs.title: Soda City Masters
clubs.description:
Soda City Masters is a USMS‑registered team in Columbia, South Carolina, welcoming swimmers aged 18 and up; whether you're chasing fitness, honing your stroke, or simply want to swim with a friendly crew.

Founded in 2025, our club believes adult swimming is less about lap counts and more about community: high‑fives, laughter, and making progress measured in both technique and camaraderie. Fun is our guiding star, but our practices are structured and supported with on‑deck coaching so every swimmer learns, adapts, and improves.

We embrace all swimmers—from those just dipping a toe in to triathletes chasing split‑second PRs. Beyond workouts, we spark connection with social gatherings, occasional community outreach, and playful challenges that turn lanes into shared stories.

Our mission is to empower adult swimmers with structured support, inclusive fitness, and a sense of belonging that makes every stroke feel a little more like home.

Dive in with our free two-week trial—where your fitness goals and future friends are waiting at the end of the lane line.

clubs.title: Riptide Masters Swimming
clubs.description: Riptide Masters Swimming is the largest and most active masters swimming program in the greater New Orleans area offering workout locations in both New Orleans and Metairie.  We were also the first masters program in our LMSC to achieve USMS Gold Club recognition.  Our program is led by a team of seven professional USMS (2 - Level 3, 2 - Level 2, and 3 - Level 1 Certified Coaches), USAT (2 Certified Coaches), and ASCA certified coaches (1 - Level 3 Certified Coach), who are all known for their creative and challenging workouts.  Several of our coaches and swimmers actively serve on Southern Masters Swimming LMSC and US Masters Swimming boards and committees.  Our Head Coach is currently the Vice Chair of the USMS National Coaches Committee and was a featured presenter at the 2025 National USMS Annual Meeting on club development.  Riptide Masters Swimming utilizes the UNO Aquatic Center (the largest aquatic facility in the area with an indoor 8 lane, 50 meter pool and outdoor 6 lane, 25 yard pool) and St. Martin's Episcopal School (8 lane, 25 yard pool) in Metairie.  Currently, we are offering 11 workout options each week, with workouts available on all 7 days.  Whether you're looking to better your swim leg for an upcoming triathlon, stay or get back into shape or learn champion-tested techniques from a caring coaching staff, Riptide Masters is your alternative to swimming incorrectly or swimming alone.   Our workouts and training program are designed to accommodate and challenge swimmers of all ability levels (from national champions through beginning fitness swimmers).  Simply put, our goal is to continue to set the standard for masters swimming in Louisiana and Mississippi!  We look forward to having you join us...

clubs.title: Greenville Splash Masters
clubs.description:
Greenville Splash Masters is a USMS Masters swim team in Greenville, South Carolina. The team was created and founded in 2009 by the vision and passion of several local swimmers. Our mission is to improve adult fitness and health through the camaraderie of swimming. We are a very active club that does many community service projects, provides coached workouts in the pool and in open water and has several team socials and events throughout the year. Greenville Splash was awarded Club of the Year in 2018 by USMS.

The Greenville Splash Masters Swim program is an adult fitness swimming program. We offer anyone 18 years of age or older a fun and safe way to improve physical and mental well being through swimming. All practices have an experienced Masters Coach on deck who provides expert assistance in training technique, stroke mechanics, and fitness goal setting. Each workout is an hour long and accommodates all skill levels: beginner, intermediate, advanced, or triathlete.

Swimming is a very technical and athletic skill. Our coaches will help swimmers achieve success with their fitness goals by improving their endurance, strength, technique and efficiency. Learning and acquiring swimming skills takes time and patience. We welcome swimmers to join us on their journey to stay fit and healthy through our workouts.

clubs.description:

Based out of the University of Sewanee's pool, our club is dedicated to promoting fitness, camaraderie, and competitive swimming among adults of all skill levels. What We Offer:
Community: We're building a vibrant community of swimmers who share a passion for swimming and fitness. 
Competitive Opportunities: Participate in local, regional, and national swim meets sanctioned by US Masters Swimming. Challenge yourself and celebrate achievements with our team.

clubs.title: Knoxville Open Water Swimmers
clubs.description: A group for experienced open water swimmers, swimming in the Tennessee River, open to boat traffic (including large freight barges), and subject to highly variable conditions: flow, water temperature and wind. You must be able to swim at least a 2:15/100m swim pace. This is not an entry-level swim. This should NOT be your first time swimming in open water.
clubs.practiceTimes:
Thursdays evenings, typically late April - early October, weather permitting. Arrive by 6:00 pm to sign in. Mandatory safety briefing at 6:15 pm, in water 6:30 - 7:30 pm. Times may change with sunset. Updates via private Facebook group.
Swimmers must wear a bright swim cap and are encouraged to wear an inflatable tow buoy. Wetsuits are permitted.
Pilots on kayaks or paddle boards are always welcome. Swimmers are expected to pilot two times per season.
*Please contact us before attending, in case conditions force a different route or cancelation.

clubs.description:
Poseidon Swimming is a USA Swimming Bronze level competitive club swim team. Poseidon has been fostering excellence in swimming in the Richmond area for over 30 years. It is a 501(3)c organization run by a parent-led Board of Directors and Head Coach, Ted Sallade.
The Poseidon Masters Swimming program is run by Coach Justin Rich, a former Poseidon and Virginia Tech swimmer. Masters Swimming is a coached program for adults 18 and older who are working to strengthen swim skills and endurance for fitness, competition or personal enjoyment. Masters Swim runs in the morning Monday through Friday. You’ll spend 55 minute in the pool swimming laps, and your coach will be available to develop workouts and offer suggestions to improve the mechanics of your strokes and your efficiency in the water.

clubs.title: NOVA of Virginia Aquatics
clubs.description:
NOVA of Virginia Masters swimming program is a USMS Gold Club that serves swimmers age 19 and over who wish to swim in a structured training program. NOVA Masters is the home of World Aquatics Masters World Top 10 swimmers, multiple USMS National Champions, very highly achieved triathletes, and swimmers who love the sport and enjoy the team's camaraderie.

Practices are coached by Mark Kutz, a USA Swimming Level 4 certified coach with over 20 years of experience coaching masters swimmers. Mark was 2012's USMS Kerry O'Brien award winner, which is given in recognition of coaches who have done a great job in building strong USMS membership in local communities. Mark's enthusiasm and attentiveness as a coach bring a fantastic pool deck atmosphere like no other.

Practices are M-F at 6 am, as well as MWF at 8:30 am. The early morning practices are fast paced, high intensity training. The later morning practices typically do the same workouts at a more moderate pace. Please contact us if you're interested in joining and with any questions you might have!

clubs.title: New Albany Narwhals
clubs.description:
Ready to make a splash and connect with a vibrant community? Our Masters Swim Team is a welcoming and affirming space specifically designed for LGBTQIA+ swimmers of all levels and experiences. Whether you're a seasoned competitor, a returning swimmer, or just looking to improve your fitness and connect with like-minded individuals, you'll find your home with us.

We believe that everyone deserves to feel comfortable and celebrated in the water. Our team fosters an environment of inclusivity, respect, and camaraderie, where you can be your authentic self while enjoying the many benefits of swimming. We focus on technique improvement, endurance building, and fun, supportive workouts tailored to a diverse range of abilities.

Join us for invigorating practices, social events, and the opportunity to forge lasting friendships. Come as you are, and let's make waves together!

clubs.title: Swim Houston Aquatics Center Masters
clubs.description: There’s exciting news for Houston area swimmers! Long-time Gulf swim coaches Gilbert and Karol Legaspi have just announced their new USA Swimming approved club, Swim Houston Aquatics Center (ShAC). The ShAC team will practice year-round at its unique state-of-the-art facility, which boasts a nine-lane heated outdoor competition pool, with six 25-yard short course lanes and three 50-meter long course lanes (which will allow for year-round long course training!)at 6'6' deep across. The ShAC competition pool is adjacent to ShAC’s indoor teaching and aquatic therapy pools. ShAC is the first coach-owned facilty and run USA Swimming approved club in the Greater Houston Metropolitan Area. ShAC is located on three grassy acres of land in Southwest Houston, with easy access for swimmers living in Houston, as well as swimmers from neighboring suburbs, such as Sugar Land, Pearland, Stafford and Missouri City.

clubs.title: Woodlands Masters Swim Team
clubs.description:
Woodlands Masters Swim Team (WMST) is the one of the largest and one of the longest running teams in the Gulf LMSC. We have grown substantially since our origin in 1987 and we currently have over 180 active members. 
At Woodlands Masters Swim Team, we welcome adult swimmers of all abilities and ages. We have swimmers of all levels who compete, swim just for fitness, and we also have many members who are triathletes working on the swimming leg of their race. Every workout has on-deck coaching from one of our twelve highly qualified coaches. WMST practices out of two great swimming venues, the Conroe ISD Natatorium and the Northampton Center and we offer 16 workouts with sessions scheduled every day of the week. With that many workouts, it is easy to find options that will fit any swimmer’s schedule. We also offer prospective new members a free two-week trial period.
We regularly host South Central Zone meets, Gulf swim meets, and we have also hosted 4 USMS National Swim Meets.
No masters team would be complete without an active social aspect, and WMST is no exception. Annual events include a BBQ in the summer and the Christmas party each December. And, of course, there are the post-competition and monthly post-practice gatherings for food, drink, and fun.
If you live in the Woodlands/Spring area, check out Woodlands Masters at www.wmst.net and find a workout time that’s perfect for you. Take advantage of our two-week free trial period and find a new swimming home with WMST.  To read more about our two-week free trial and signing up, please visit:  https://wmst.net/join/free-two-week-trial

clubs.title: Right Duff
clubs.description:
Anne Duffy is an ALTS (Adult-Learn-to-Swim) instructor, as well as a U.S. Masters Level I & II- certified swim coach. 
She specializes in working with beginner swimmer adults who may be very nervous in the water, yet want to begin swimming for health, fitness, being with their children, and many other goals. Anne is also a USA Triathlon Level I-certified coach and works with beginner & intermediate triathletes on improving their swim strokes. Other triathlon-swim-related skills addressed include endurance (air exchange and breathing) in the water, strength and speed, freestyle technique, and the open-water skills needed for the swimming portion of the triathlon.
clubs.practiceTimes:

Available times are at Arlington County pools in the early mornings (5:30-8:30am) weekdays, or on weekends.

clubs.title: Club Tribe
clubs.description:
Club Tribe is a U.S. Masters swimming team in the Virginia LMSC, founded in 2014 by a few former William & Mary swimmers who just wanted to swim relays together at USMS meets. We currently have approximately 200 swimmers, ranging from 18 to 80+ years old.
We currently have training groups in Arlington, Fairfax, Winchester, Blacksburg, Rockville MD, and even Anchorage AK!
Approximately half of our team members have prior affiliations with William & Mary, but this is by no means a requirement to join.
Team members pay a $75 annual club membership fee that covers Coach Rich’s virtual coaching, relays and other meet costs, and other team expenses.

clubs.description:
Welcome to the Masters section of our USA Swimming team: Elite Rays Swim Club (ERSC). We are proud that you are joining our Masters to be part of one of the most dynamic, challenge-seeking, and stress-free groups of swim-loving adults in our area. Within the ERSC family, you will pursue and meet your athletic goals as well as set new ones under the expert eye of our coaches and the unwavering support of your teammates and our social network. ERSC closely guides your progression matching you with other swimmers with similar abilities and objectives to maximize each phase of your development as a swimmer. Getting started is exciting, but we take it upon ourselves to maintain your enthusiasm year long.  

To sign up quickly and easily, please follow the online registration steps described below. Please read carefully to the end to make sure you understand the latest changes. If you have any problems or need assistance, do not hesitate to contact us anytime at admin@eliteraysswim.com

clubs.title: LOUD Loons
clubs.description: The LOUD Loons are located in Loudoun County, Virginia. LOUD for Loudoun! We are a very chill team for those swimmers or triathletes who want a group of people to workout with and attend races with. We are not organized through Ida Lee, but most of us do swim there. Weekly workouts will be emailed out with various levels to choose from so you can modify as needed. If you want to write one of the weekly workouts, let Rachel Pumphrey know. Swim on your own or with teammates as schedules allow. Will have an email chain where we can communicate when we all plan to swim that week so that we can meet up as much as possible. Grab lane space and circle swim with as many teammates as you can! This team will not ever be reserving lane space or have a coach on deck. These two factors complicate things very quickly. No formal recruiting process. Just word of mouth. Invite your friends that you think would enjoy this.

clubs.title: SUWC Wahoos
clubs.description:
SUWC Wahoos Masters Swim Club offers adult swim programs for all levels. Our U.S. Masters Swim Practices are coach-led workouts focused on endurance, technique, speed, and race prep—perfect for beginning to advanced fitness swimmers, triathletes, and competitors. Practices are available midday and evenings, with early morning and weekend options coming soon. Month-to-month registration includes multiple weekly sessions and is geared toward intermediate to advanced swimmers.

For beginners, our Adult Learn-to-Swim classes provide a supportive space to build water safety, floating, breathing, and basic stroke skills. Classes run twice weekly in the evenings for 8 sessions.

Stroke Technique Clinics are ideal for beginner to intermediate lap swimmers looking to improve form. Each 4-class session focuses on a specific stroke, with drills and individual feedback.
Email Coach Beccah at rnelson@shepherd.edu

clubs.title: JCC Masters Swim Team
clubs.description:
The JCC of Greater Columbus Masters Swim Team is a welcoming and supportive community for adult swimmers of all experience levels. Whether you’re returning to the pool, training for competition, or looking for a motivating group to stay active with, our Masters program offers structured workouts led by knowledgeable coaches who prioritize technique, fitness, and fun.

Our team trains year-round in our newly renovated aquatics facilities, with practices designed to meet the needs of both competitive and fitness-focused swimmers. We emphasize personal improvement, building confidence in the water, and fostering strong connections among teammates.

Come swim with us, challenge yourself, and be part of a positive, energetic swim community. All adults 18+ are welcome—no competitive experience required.
clubs.practiceTimes: Our Masters practices are structured, coach-led sessions designed to improve stroke technique, build endurance, and enhance overall swimming performance. Each workout includes a warm-up, skill-specific drills, a main training set, and a cooldown. Practices are tailored to accommodate a range of abilities, allowing both competitive and fitness-focused swimmers to train at an appropriate and challenging level. Coaches provide guidance, feedback, and motivation throughout each session.

clubs.title: Merritt Marlins
clubs.description:
Masters swimming is a great way to learn proper stroke technique and efficiency,injury prevention, and workout strategies. Our program focuses on helping participants set and meet individual goals. Whether you are a novice, a lap swimmer, or current competitive swimmer, or if you would like to improve your swim skills in the four competitive strokes, you will find yourself learning and developing your fitness in the masters swim program. Everyone will receive coaching based on their goals and interests. If you enjoy a structured workout,technical feedback, and meeting new swimmers, you should consider our
Masters program.
The workouts are periodized, meaning that the technique emphasis and the conditioning emphasis varies every 6 weeks. Periodization is a key aspect of training at the highest levels of sport and has been shown to give better results than other training strategies.
The program also includes monthly standard performance tests. These tests are part of a normal workout and allow both the coaches and the swimmers to know whether they are improving.

In addition to elite triathlons and open water races, French Creek Racing holds open water training swims, and indoor/outdoor swim training year round. Our high quality training and attention to the details that matter to you as the athlete are what set us apart from everyone else in the game. We have a team of amazing coaches, and support staff to give you the best experiences at our events, and trainings. We know every athlete has unique goals, and we’re here to help you achieve them quickly, affordably, and while having fun along the way!

clubs.title: O2 Performance Aquatics
clubs.description:
O2 Masters is an adult program (age 18 and over) designed to train the recreational, fitness-minded, competitive and triathlete swimmer.  Professional coaches prepare and administer the workouts specifically designed for the adult swimmer and their goals.  The Coaching philosophy is Speed is Achieved First through Technique and then followed by Aerobic / Anaerobic Endurance Training.   

The composition of the team is such that you will find at least 2-5 athletes who have similar swimming abilities as you and share your passion for the sport.

O2 Masters attends numerous competitive events each year. For competitive swimmers, there are several local swim meets sponsored by North Texas Masters. In addition, the team participates in United States Masters National Swimming Championships, both Pool and Open Water events. For the Triathlete, in addition to their USTA competition schedule, the team attends several Open Water events, including Swim Across America and the annual Lake Travis Relays held in Austin, TX.  For the Fitness swimmer, the team participates in USMS sponsored events such as the One Hour Postal Swim, the and DAM SMU Christmas Relays

The team also holds regular social events throughout the year.

clubs.title: Nitro Swimming
clubs.description:
At Nitro, our Masters Swimming Program is open to all adults who are dedicated to improving their fitness, training for specific goals and enjoying the camaraderie of other swimmers. Our Masters group provides workouts which are designed to help you improve your performance over time.

There is a set of watchful eyes on the deck during practice times to help you as much (or as little) as you’d like.

So whether you are just starting on your swim journey and your goal is to learn to swim better for health or fitness, or looking to complete your first triathlon, or perhaps you’re on the upper end of the swimming scale and you want to compete at Masters Nationals, you’ll find what you need here at Nitro.

The Masters' workouts are typically in the range of 3000-4000 yards in 75 minutes, but we adjust that accordingly for whatever your level is, and make things comfortable and enjoyable for you.
clubs.practiceTimes: Morning practices are offered at Bee Cave and Cedar Park, and we also offer a lunch time practice several days a week at Bee Cave.

Some members focus on local, regional, national or international swimming  competitions and/or triathlons. Others swim to keep fit. The Philadelphia Fins Aquatics Club was founded in 1988 for LGBTQ+ swimmers in the Philadelphia area and remains Philadelphia's premier LGBTQ+ masters swim team.  We are a member of United States Masters Swimming (USMS) and International Gay and Lesbian Aquatics (IGLA) organizations. clubs.title: Alamo Area Masters at Alamo Heights - AAMAH
clubs.description: We welcome all registered USMS swimmers at daily practices, M-F, from 5:00-7:00 a.m. with our USMS Level 3 Head Coach, Allison LaField.  AAMAH also has  several USMS Level 1 coaches readily available to offer in water assistance everyday of the week. Being a AAMAH member  also allows you use of our pool from 1030am-1:30pm, M-F and Sat for lap swimming.  Our pool is a 4 yr old, state of the art, indoor facility.  We have 13 lanes each 25 yards long.  The cost to join our team is $75.00 a month as an AAMAH registered team member. We offer a military/veteran/first responder discount, as a thank you for their service. The discounted cost is 45.00 a month. In addition to AAMAH monthly fees we ask that you join USMS upon completing the free trial period. We always welcome fellow USMS out of town swimmers visiting the Alamo City to drop in for a practice with us,  free of charge.
clubs.practiceTimes:
AAMAH practices are individually geared towards the swimmers abilities. Some of our athletes have been swimming for years, competitively or for fitness, some are triathletes, and others are brand new to the sport or just learning to swim for the first time in our Learn To Swim Program.  We also cater to the military including active/retired and those with injuries or amputations, and those training for or just beginning their service to our country. We also welcome all first responders.
We have a unique, cohesive and diverse group of swimmers who exude acceptance, positivity and motivate each other to reach individual improvement goals, while sharing in each others individual successes. Our group ranges from an Olympic Paratriathlete to adults conquering their life long fear of the water.

clubs.title: Central Illinois Masters
clubs.description: The Central Illinois Masters Swim Team, Inc. (CIMST) is a non-profit US Masters Swimming (USMS) sanctioned swim team.  Our mission is to promote physical fitness, mental well-being, and camaraderie through swimming at all levels.  We provide a pool-based training program for adults (minimum age 18, with no maximum) focused on proper swimming technique and fitness.
clubs.practiceTimes: Practices are held at the Shirk Center on the Illinois Wesleyan University campus and at other pools in the community.  Please go to the team's website, cimst.org, for the current practice schedule and pools.

clubs.title: Masters of Streamline Aquatics
clubs.description:
Masters of Streamline Aquatics is a coached adult swim program designed for swimmers of all backgrounds and experience levels. Whether you are new to the sport, returning after time away, training for fitness, open water, triathlon, or competitive Masters swimming, our program provides structured workouts, technical instruction, and a supportive team environment.

Our focus is on smart training, strong fundamentals, and long-term development. Practices are organized by ability and goals, ensuring each swimmer receives an appropriate challenge while building confidence, efficiency, and consistency in the water. Masters of Streamline Aquatics promotes health, community, and performance—both in and out of the pool.
clubs.practiceTimes:
Masters of Streamline Aquatics offers coached practices year-round with structured training plans that balance technique, endurance, and speed. Practices are led by experienced coaches and organized into lanes based on ability, experience, and training goals.

Training environments include:

University of the Incarnate Word – Ann Barshop Natatorium: A collegiate-level indoor facility providing a controlled, high-quality training environment.

Open Water Opportunities (seasonal): Structured open water sessions designed to build confidence, navigation skills, and endurance for open water and triathlon athletes.

Typical practices include:

Dynamic warm-ups and skill development

Technique-focused drills and stroke refinement

Aerobic and anaerobic main sets

Optional race preparation and pacing work

Swimmers may attend practices flexibly based on their schedules, and lane placement may adjust as fitness and goals evolve. All workouts are scalable to ensure safety, progression, and enjoyment for every athlete.

clubs.title: River Crossing - Bethlehem YMCA
clubs.description: We are an adult Masters Swimming program that works out at River Crossing - Bethlehem YMCA. Our group is a mix of pure swimmers and endurance athletes across a broad spectrum of ability levels ranging from beginner to seasoned racer. Our pool is an indoor chlorinated pool with 4 lanes and certified to 25 SCY. We officially go: Tuesdays and Thursdays, 0515 - 0700.
clubs.practiceTimes:
Our current weekly workout schedule is: 
Tuesdays and Thursdays: 0515 - 0700
If you decide to get in the water with us, please observe the following:
- The Bethlehem YMCA facility opens at 0500, Monday - Friday. 
- All swimmers must rinse/shower prior to entering the pool.
- Doors to the pool from the locker rooms open at 0510 (on my watch, not any one of the multiple clocks reading varying times around the facility). 
- Doors to the pool from the locker rooms re-lock at 0520 (I do not answer doors being pulled upon at/after 0521).
- If attending Masters, but running late, please use the main pool door at the end of the Family Locker Room hallway to gain entry to the pool from 0521 - 0700; if I am present, this door is open.

What to bring:
1. Swim cap(preferably silicone)
2. Swim goggles(preferably swedes-style and NOT mirrored. We are an indoor pool with low-light conditions prior to sunrise.  Please wear goggles that maximize safe vision and situational awareness while working out in potentially-crowded lanes.)
3. Proper swim attire
4. Fins, preferably:
https://www.swimoutlet.com/products/finis-zoomers-gold-swim-fins-1267/?color=gold
5. Hand paddles, preferably:
https://www.swimoutlet.com/products/finis-manta-swim-paddles-8210846/?color=yellow

There are no minimum requirements for skills, speed, yardage, etc., to join us and see if what we do is the right fit and providing the right guidance you seek to get on and stay on your current fitness goals trajectory.

clubs.title: Aqua Masters
clubs.description: Masters swimming is an organized program of swimming for adults 18 and older. Members participate in a variety of ways ranging from lap swimming to triathlons. Our Club is a sanctioned U.S. Masters Swimming program. Our official program/club name is: Aqua Masters (AM). Anyone 18 or over can join Masters  swimming, and anyone 19 and over may compete. U.S. Masters Swimming has over 25,000 members, a few of whom are in there 90's. Members include: Post-college swimmers, former high school and area age group swimmers, local triathletes and adult fitness swimmers. Craig Culver, former NCAA collegiate swimmer and high school swimming coach, certified USMS masters swimming coach, American Swimming Coaches Association Swim Coach, Seven time United States Triathlon National Championship qualifier, 5 time Ironman finisher including Hawaii Ironman World Championship Finisher. Craig started the Bradley-Bourbonnais Swim Club (BBSC) in 1982 and the Bradley-Bourbonnais Masters Aquatics (BBMA) in 2013. The Masters program is restarting after a long COVID break. The new club will be called "Aqua Masters".  You can be a member and workout everywhere! However, for the purpose of our program/club, we are currently conducting organized, structured, regular workouts at Kankakee High School: 1200 West Jeffery Street, Kankakee, IL 60901. On deck coaching/workouts will be provided on the following days/times: Monday and Wednesday evenings 5:30pm-7:00pm. Additional written workouts will be emailed to members for their own use each week.  Also, many members are multi-sport athletes, such as Triathletes. The Swimming program provides Triathletes a structure for enhancing their swimming skills. But if competing isn't your style, there's no need to feel pressured. Many Masters swimmers are simply interested in the regular routine of working out. Free Consultation available. Are you considering joining the AM? Competing in your first swim meet or triathlon? Perhaps you are an experienced swimmer or triathlete that has reached a plateau? Contact Culvertraining@gmail.com and sign-up for a free consultation. We stand behind our commitment to provide our athletes with the best customer service. We promise to return all communications within 48 hours or less. Optimizing training success is our number one priority. Contact Craig at: culvertraining@gmail.com session Membership. Private and group supplemental workout, training and stroke analysis and instruction available contact: Craig Culver at Culvertraining@gmail.com for details or at 815.263.0105.

clubs.title: Train Daly
clubs.description: Train Daly Masters is a community of open water, triathlete, and fitness enthusiasts, lead by former collegiate all-American and strength and conditioning coaches, specializing in technical efficiency, dryland strength, and underwater video analysis, to help every athlete swim strong.
clubs.practiceTimes:
Practices are held at John Jay College Pool on W59th St in Manhattan. The John Jay Swimming Pool is 25-yard competition pool with five lanes. The depth of the pool ranges from 4 feet to 13.4 feet, equipped with starting blocks, and digital pace clocks. Pull buoys and kick board are available for use. Personal fins (https://amzn.to/3E0FZnP) and paddles (https://amzn.to/3BPfoHm) are recommended.

*Important*

To participate in practice you must be a registered USMS.org member. 

If you are not yet an annual USMS.org member, please complete using the links below. Choose LMSC "Metropolitan", Team "Train Daly (TDALY)"

Registered with another team? No problem. Please forward your USMS number to Dan@TrainDaly.com prior to practice.

USMS Annual Membership (https://www.usms.org/join-usms/join-or-renew)

30 Day Trial Membership (https://www.usms.org/volunteer-central/guide-to-local-operations/lmsc-operations/registration/registration-procedures/trial-membership)

Foreign Guest Membership (https://www.usms.org/volunteer-central/guide-to-local-operations/lmsc-operations/registration/registration-procedures/foreign-guest)

Practice Details

John Jay College Pool
524 West 59th Street, New York, NY 10019

Coach Dan Daly, CSCS
TrainDaly.com
347-850-DALY 


Venue Directions: 

The John Jay Swimming Pool is located on the 'C' level of Haaren Hall. The facility consists of a 25-yard pool with five lanes. The depth of the pool ranges from 4 feet to 13.4 feet. 

Adjacent to the pool, there are men's and women's locker rooms that are available to all swimmers and teams.

Pool Rules

In Compliance with CUNY and the New York City Board of Health: 

1)The pool deck will not be open until the rental time begins. Participants can change and utilize the locker room institutions prior to their time in the pool.

2)There is a zero street shoe policy on deck. This includes coaches.

3)All participants must shower prior to entering the pool.

4) Cotton materials are not allowed as swim suits.

5)Swim caps must be worn by all bathers unless clean shaved. (Train Daly team caps are available for all swimmers)

6) Food or beverages other than water are NOT permitted on deck or the locker rooms. Please note: This includes fruit, snacks, or nuts.

7) John Jay College is not responsible for lost personal belongings brought to the facility or locker rooms. Anything left over after the designated rental time will be discarded. 

8.) CUNY’s COVID-19 Campus Safety Policy re Visitor Access to Campus (“Visitor Policy”) which provides that every visitor to a CUNY campus, whether accessing indoor or outdoor spaces, must provide proof to CUNY that they (i) are fully vaccinated or (ii) have had a negative COVID-19 molecular (PCR) test performed by an accredited lab no more than 7 days prior to the visit. Please allow additional time on your first visit for clearing security.

clubs.title: FAST
clubs.description:
FAST is a Southeast Michigan based non-profit group of athletes who participate in swimming and triathlons for recreation, health, fitness, and competition. We have members with diverse abilities, experience, and motivation, ranging from recreational swimmers to veterans of Ironman distance triathlons.

FAST was formed in 1984 by a small group of swimmers at Ford Motor Co. who got together to train and compete in local Masters swim meets. Within two years, the Club expanded to include the fast growing sport of triathlon. Membership has swelled each year to its current level of about 200. FAST members can be found throughout and beyond Southeast Michigan.

Our two major focuses are broken down: September to March – swimming; April to August – triathlons. However, they overlap so much that we get members who train all year round for both swimming and/or triathlons.

FAST organizes swim practice sessions throughout the year, always well attended by expert and novice swimmers alike. FAST swimmers can frequently be found competing at local Masters swim meets as well. The club is actively involved in triathlon competition throughout the world, from sprint to Ironman distance events. In 2003, we organized a new sprint distance race in Novi, Michigan and it has been a success ever since.

We tie people together through the web, e-mails and social media. If someone wants more coaching while swimming, we put them in touch with our swimming gurus. If they want to learn how to run in a marathon, we have members who teach classes on that. If you want to talk to other experienced master swimmers or age group triathletes, we line that up too. You want it, we probably have tried it and can do it again!

Whatever your fitness goals, FAST can help you meet or exceed them. Some of the benefits of joining the club include: discounts, organized training groups, social events such as our yearly holiday party, season kick-off BBQ, and the occasional happy hour, Masters swimming relays, Saturday swim practices during the cold months, and last but not least, great friendships!

clubs.title: Chicago Blue Dolphins
clubs.description:
Chicago Blue Dolphins' PACE Swim Training (Precision Aquatic Conditioning & Education) is a Technique + Training Lab for Adult Swimmers based in Chicago.
PACE helps adult swimmers improve both how they swim and how they train through a combination of in-person technique coaching and structured swim training delivered through the PACE online community.
Our in-person coaching focuses on stroke instruction and video analysis, helping swimmers improve efficiency, body position, and timing through personalized feedback.
Training guidance is delivered through the PACE community, where swimmers receive structured weekly practices and coaching insights designed to support fitness swimmers, triathletes, and Masters athletes.
Upcoming PACE training plans will also support swimmers preparing for Masters competitions, triathlon races, and open-water events, providing structured progression and race-specific preparation.
PACE is designed for swimmers who want a smarter way to improve — combining modern technique coaching with structured swim training.
clubs.practiceTimes:
PACE provides structured swim practices and training guidance through the PACE online training community.
Members receive weekly swim practices organized by ability level, along with technique guidance and coaching interpretation to help swimmers improve efficiency and endurance.
Practices can be completed at the swimmer’s home pool and are designed for swimmers focusing on:
** freestyle and three-stroke technique development
** overall swim fitness
** Masters swimming
** triathlon preparation
** open-water swimming
In-person coaching in Chicago focuses on stroke instruction and video analysis, allowing swimmers to refine technique while following structured training practices independently.
Future PACE training plans will include race-specific preparation for Masters meets, triathlon events, and open-water races, expanding the program’s ability to support swimmers pursuing performance goals.

clubs.title: Open Water Swim Team
clubs.description:
Open Water Swim (OWS) is committed to teaching beginners to learn how to swim as well as professional athletes with the same basic theories that will enable each individual to swim faster and easier by using proven techniques established by Coach Bryan and his professional coaching staff with over 20 years coaching experience.

OWS consists of several training tactics that will assist anyone competing in open water swims, those looking to compete in the pool races and those who just want to learn how to swim and feel more comfortable in the water. After a few classes you will start to feel more confident, stronger, and most importantly relaxed when faced with the challenge of swimming in an open water competition. For those that are already expert swimmers or those just starting out, OWS will give you the tools, skills and mindset that will carry over into your race day.

The objective of the Open Water Swim Clinic is to provide all types of swimmers (novice/beginner, intermediate, expert) with information and proven swim techniques, tactics and drills to assist you in your training and prepare you for open water swims and competitions with the end goal of making you faster and more comfortable in the water.
I have a proven track record, successfully working with swimmers that couldn’t swim more than five strokes without major difficulty or distress. I have trained these same people to successfully compete in open water races such as triathlons, the Cross Bay Swim and many other distance swim races. 
I have become so inspired and driven by these successes and incredible achievements, that I have committed myself to sharing my extensive knowledge and experience beyond my Open Water Masters Swim program on Long Island so that I can continue to make even a greater difference in the world of swimming and open water swim competitions. This OWS Clinic is conducted in a pool as well as an open water environment (depending on weather), and is intended to provide swimmers and aspiring athletes with the tools they need to improve their swimming technique, make them more comfortable in the water and prepare them for any future open water swim challenges. I guarantee that if you train with me, you will be a successful swimmer.

clubs.title: Flying Carp Swimming
clubs.description:
Flying Carp swimmers swim at skill levels from beginning and fitness swimmers to highly competitive swimmers, from late bloomers to former age group and collegiate swimmers, and in ages from their early twenties and up. Swimmers should be able to swim freestyle unaided for the length of the pool, although arrangements can be made for those persons who desire to learn to swim correctly and safely. 
The Flying Carp symbolizes courage and ambition.  The carp is a traditional Asian symbol of strength, endurance and tenacity.  The image is also of a real carp fighting its way upstream to spawn, representing energy and health.  The original Chinese story is that if a carp successfully swam up the Yellow River and climbed the waterfall to the source of the river, it became a dragon. 
Thus our motto:  Farther and Faster

clubs.title: Orion Recreation Center
clubs.description:
The Orion Recreation Center (ORC) offers a wide range of health, fitness, and recreation opportunities for all ages and ability levels. Our facility features a fully equipped cardio and strength floor, indoor track, Olympic weightlifting area, multiple fitness studios, and two full-size basketball courts. Members can also enjoy group exercise classes, personal training, and wellness programs designed to help you reach your fitness goals.
Beyond fitness, ORC is a community hub with spaces for creativity, connection, and learning. Our Art Room hosts a variety of arts and crafts programs for youth and adults, while the Multipurpose Room accommodates events, meetings, and community gatherings. The 50+ Senior Wing provides a welcoming space for older adults to socialize, participate in specialized programs, and stay active.
Our indoor saltwater-chlorinated pool features six 25-yard lanes, a designated space for water aerobics, and fun family amenities like a water bubble and umbrella waterfall. The pool depth ranges from 1 foot to 5.5 feet, and we maintain a comfortable temperature between 82–84°F. The seasonal outdoor pool includes a water slide, diving area, lap lanes, and a zero-depth entry perfect for younger swimmers. With depths ranging from 0 to 12.5 feet and water temperatures between 78–82°F, it’s the ideal spot for both relaxation and recreation.
Memberships are open to residents and non-residents, with options for monthly, annual, and summer memberships, as well as day passes. Whether you’re looking to stay active, spend time with family, explore your creativity, or connect with the community, the Orion Recreation Center is your destination for fitness and fun in Orion Township.

clubs.title: Werl Pool
clubs.description: WerlPool Masters is a seasonal program that trains in an outdoor, three-lane, 25-yard pool in North Stamford, from May through early October. The pool features a perimeter gutter system with lanes that are 7 feet wide and a depth that descends from five to seven feet deep. We have a digital pace clock, starting blocks, backstroke flags, regulation lane markers, and above-ground and underwater speakers. The water is heated to a consistent 77 but gets warmer in the summer. There’s also a pool house for showers and changing. Practices are typically in the morning, before work. Tri-athletes are welcome. Un-coached swimming is available in the afternoon or evening.
clubs.practiceTimes: We welcome swimmers of all ages and genders. Most of us are swimming for fitness, although some compete in pools, open water races or triathlons. We are self-coached with fresh workouts written for every practice. We typically host five practices per week (Tues, Wed, Fri, Sat and Sun), lasting an hour and a half, with an average distance of 4,400 per workout. Each practice usually has a warm-up, main set, kick set, pull set and a cool down. At least once a week we focus on non-freestyle strokes in the main set. Weekday practices typically begin between 6:15 - 6:30am and weekends start around 8:00am. All interested swimmers are asked to join our WhatsApp chat to see the daily schedule updates.

clubs.title: Ridgefield Aquatic Club Masters
clubs.description:
Get Fit. Swim Strong. Join the RAC Masters Community!

About the Program
The Ridgefield Aquatic Club Masters Program is designed for adult swimmers of all levels—from fitness swimmers to competitive athletes. Whether you want to improve your technique, train for a triathlon, or stay fit, our program offers a supportive and structured environment.

Who Can Join?
Adults (18+) of all skill levels  
Fitness enthusiasts  
Former competitive swimmers  
Triathletes looking for swim-specific training  
New swimmers looking to learn proper technique and build endurance

Program Highlights
Structured Workouts: Professionally designed workouts catering to different skill levels and goals, with a balance of endurance, speed, and technique training.  
Experienced Coaches: Our professional coaching staff provides personalized feedback to help you improve your strokes, build strength, and increase endurance.  
Flexible Schedule: Multiple practice times are offered each week, so it’s easy to find a session that fits your schedule.  
Community Atmosphere: Join a welcoming, motivated group that supports each other in reaching fitness goals.  

Training Focus Areas
Technique improvement (all strokes)  
Endurance and speed work  
Open-water swimming skills  
Starts and turns  
Race strategies for competitive swimmers
clubs.practiceTimes:
Practice Schedule
Monday, Wednesday, Friday: 
Masters 1 - 5:30 AM – 6:30 AM
Masters 2 - 6:40 AM - 7:40 AM  
Tuesday and Thursday: 
Mid-Morning Masters - 9:00 AM - 10:00 AM  

*All practices are held at the Ridgefield Aquatic Club at the Barlow Mountain School (Pool Area), equipped with state-of-the-art facilities.

clubs.title: Fort Leavenworth Lancers
clubs.description: Since 1965, we have been developing strong swimmers with a focus on stoke proficiency, conditioning, work ethic, and sportsmanship. Though our program started out working with kids, we have since grown to encompass adults as well.
clubs.practiceTimes:
WHO: All adults who like swimming, want to improve their swimming, and/or are looking to achieve fitness goals realted to swimming.

WHY: Why pay for a masters program when you can do open lap swim? Great question, with the Masters Program (1) there is a coach on deck that (2) provides you with a workout, (3) gives you individual feedback on your strokes & technique, (4) can tailor the workout to fit your needs - whether you're just starting out, looking to learn new stroke, or improve your swim time for an event, (5) working out in a group increases accountability and gives you healthy competiton, and (6) gives you the opportunity to compete at Masters monthly swim meets.
WHERE & WHEN: Harney Pool - MON, WED, FRI 5:30-7am or 8:30-10am

clubs.title: Wallingford Family YMCA Dolphins
clubs.description: The Wallingford Family YMCA Dolphins is a registered and approved US Masters Swimming Program that is organized for the purpose of cultivating the enjoyment of swimming for adults (18+) of all skill levels and abilities. Members may work out in one or more training groups (competition, fitness, social, developmental), usually under the direction of a USMS Certified Coach, or on their own through customized training plans. Members join for fun, fitness, skill development and improvement, and have the opportunity to compete locally, regionally, and nationally in age group swim meets both in-person and virtually. The WFYO also offers Adult Learn-To-Swim (ALTS) classes for non-swimmers, stroke development for swimmers looking to improve their skills, training and coaching for triathletes and open water swimmers, and dry-land training for swimmers looking to stay fit - both in and out of season.

clubs.title: J-Hawk Aquatic Club Masters
clubs.description:
We have recently started our Masters team and practice at Franklin Middle School, in Janesville.  Starting June 8th, we'll have practice options at Parker HS (Janesville), Rockport Pool (Janesville), and UW-Whitewater.  
The pools will be shared with our Mini-Hawk youth team group.  We think this is a great fit for our newer swimmers to see this is a life-long sport/activity.  We hope to help adult swimmers achieve their swimming goals for all things swimming (fitness, triathlons, or Masters swim meets).  Different workouts are written for open water and pool racer athletes which are geared for your personal goals.  Coaches will correct technique throughout the practices, as well.
Steps:
1. Register with United States Masters Swimming.
2. Register for Wisconsin Masters then select J-Hawk Masters for your workout group.
3. Register for J-Hawks on J-Hawks team page.
clubs.practiceTimes:
Location/Times:  
   Franklin Middle School in Janesville  (thru June 4th)

clubs.title: Rocket Aquatics
clubs.description: Rocket Aquatics Masters strives to maintain an environment that strikes a healthy balance between fitness, fun, and competition. All levels of adult swimmers are welcome: from fitness swimmers to competitive pool and open water swimmers, first time triathletes to the experienced Iron man finishers. RA Masters is the perfect training program for anyone who loves the swimming lifestyle regardless of your swim experience or fitness level. The role of the coaching staff is to design a program that will allow all members to reach their individual goals. We believe that improving stroke technique and providing purposeful training sets are key elements to team and individual success. We also believe that having fun is just as important!

clubs.title: McFarland Masters
clubs.description:
McFarland Masters Swim Team (Age 18&Over)

Don’t let the term Masters swimmer fool you! It doesn’t imply elite swimmers, but instead means this is a group for adult swimmers age 18 and over. The McFarland Masters Swim Team (MCFM) works on technique and endurance within a team atmosphere. The team is a diverse and deep mix of former college athletes, current triathletes, and lifelong swimmers who are new to competing. Everyone can find a lane with a good fit. The team practices most Monday, Wednesday & Friday mornings from 6-6:45 am. Meets and events are optional, but always a good time! If you are new to swimming, please join us in this safe, fun, and friendly group of adult swimmers. We work on technique, breathing, and building swim-specific strength. Everything can be adapted to best fit your skill and comfort level. Please join us!
Regular AOAC daily fees apply to each practice. Masters practices are included with your AOAC membership/pass. *To compete in official US Master swim meets/events, you need a US Masters membership. Monthly Masters schedules are available at www.mcfarlandcommunitypool.com
clubs.practiceTimes: Visit the club website for workout info: www.mcfarlandcommunitypool.com

clubs.title: Mount Wachusett Masters
clubs.description:
While the term “Masters swimming” might be unintentionally intimidating, our goal is to make sure that no one feels intimidated when joining our program. Whether you are looking to learn to swim for the first time, dust off the cobwebs after a few years of taking a break, or be a competitive swimmer; whether you just want to get a low-impact workout, get some yardage in in preparation for your triathlon, or plan to race – all are welcome and should feel welcome.

Our team prides itself on being an open, welcome community where all are willing to pitch in and help fellow teammates – camaraderie and acceptance are paramount. Secondary is our passion for quality swimming, ensuring everyone understands the “WHY” swimming is done a particular way so we all can continue to swim for years to come without injury.

We swim out of Mount Wachusett Community College year round, and have a large number of team members open water swimming in local lakes between May and September.

clubs.title: Charles River Aquatics Masters
clubs.description: The goal at Charles River Aquatics Masters is to create an enthusiastic training environment that provides mentally and physically stimulating workouts to a diverse group of athletes ages 18 and up while we adopt, align, and implement the United States Masters Swimming core objectives of Service, Educate, and Build. Whether you’re a swimmer joining a team for the first time or an Olympic medalist it the priority of Charles River Aquatics Masters to assure that each member, regardless of ability level, gets the most out of their investment and commitment to our program.
clubs.practiceTimes: Charles River Aquatics Masters offers multiple workouts each week with varying times and distances for almost any schedule. Each practice follows a daily focus to incorporate the many disciplines within competitive swimming and to help develop a well-rounded athlete. Workouts are designed to try and meet each swimmers level of fitness and speed and combine both technical drills and aerobic conditioning to promote healthy, long-term success. It is the mission of Charles River Aquatics Masters to offer an environment of excellence where visitors and members alike can reach and advance their goals through professional coaching and an atmosphere of camaraderie and support. For more details about the CRM team and practice schedule visit the team website charlesrivermasters.com

clubs.title: Glens Falls Masters
clubs.description:
The Glens Falls YMCA Masters Swimming program is a swimming program designed for adult swimmers of all skill levels, from beginner to experienced. Here's a description of what you can expect:

Program Overview

The Glens Falls YMCA Masters Swimming program is a year-round program that offers structured swimming sessions, led by an experienced USMS certified Coach. The program is designed for adult swimmers who want to improve their swimming skills, increase their fitness level, and have fun while doing it.

Swim Sessions

Swim sessions are held at the Glens Falls YMCA pool, which is a 25-yard, six-lane pool with a warm and welcoming atmosphere. Swim sessions are typically 60 minutes long and are divided into two groups:

Lap Swim: This session is designed for swimmers who want to swim laps and focus on their technique. The pace is moderate, and swimmers are encouraged to work at their own pace.
Workout Session: This session is more intense and focused on swimming drills, exercises, and games. The pace is faster, and swimmers are encouraged to challenge themselves.
Coaching and Support

The program is led by experienced coaches who provide guidance, feedback, and encouragement to help swimmers improve their technique, build endurance, and achieve their goals.

Swim Levels

The program offers three swim levels to accommodate different skill levels:

Beginner: Designed for swimmers who are new to Masters Swimming or haven't swum in a while. This level focuses on building basic swimming skills, such as freestyle, backstroke, breaststroke, and butterfly.
Intermediate: Designed for swimmers who have some experience with Masters Swimming or have already developed some basic swimming skills. This level focuses on improving technique, building endurance, and introducing more advanced swimming drills.
Advanced: Designed for experienced swimmers who want to challenge themselves and push their limits. This level focuses on advanced swimming techniques, high-intensity interval training, and goal-setting.
Membership and Pricing

Membership options vary depending on the individual's goals and needs. You can choose from a variety of membership plans, including:

Monthly Membership: A monthly membership plan that offers access to the pool, swim sessions, and other YMCA facilities.
Drop-in Fee: A daily fee option that allows you to attend a single swim session without committing to a monthly membership.
Program Package: A package deal that includes a set number of swim sessions per week or month.
Special Events and Clinics

Throughout the year, the Glens Falls YMCA Masters Swimming program offers special events and clinics, such as:

Swim Meets: Regular swim meets where swimmers can compete against each other in a fun and supportive environment.
Stroke Clinics: Specialized clinics focused on improving specific swimming strokes, such as freestyle or breaststroke.
Endurance Training: Sessions designed to help swimmers build endurance and prepare for longer swims or triathlons.
Overall, the Glens Falls YMCA Masters Swimming program offers a supportive and challenging environment for adult swimmers to improve their skills, build endurance, and have fun while doing it!
clubs.practiceTimes: Monday and Wednesday 6:15pm-7:30pm

During the Fall, Winter, And Spring, we train at the following times:

Please contact Scituate Racquet & Fitness regarding membership at their facilities: www.srfclub.com or 781-545-1184. If you pay the membership fees to the facilities, there is no extra charge to swim, nor is there any requirement that you join U.S. Masters Swimming or Wild Crab Masters Swimming. We find U.S. Masters Swimming a great resource for information and collegiality, and many of our Wild Crab swimmers swim competitively throughout the year.

clubs.title: University of Hawaii Masters Swimming
clubs.description:
University of Hawai’i Masters Swim Club is a training group for adults interested in improving their swimming speed, endurance, and technique in a fun, positive, athlete-centered environment.

We are dedicated to helping swimmers of all ability levels improve their swimming skills. We do interval training and stroke technique drills to help accelerate your swim training for triathlons, open water swims, fitness, and everything in between. Each workout is divided into training groups for specialized workouts that will take you to the next level.

Follow our NEW Instagram page at https://www.instagram.com/uhmasters/ 

The Duke Kahanamoku Aquatic Complex (DKAC) serves as the home for the University of Hawai‘i’s swimming and diving, women’s water polo, and UH Masters swimming teams. The facility, located on the University’s lower campus, includes a 50-meter training pool and a separate 25-yard competition and diving pool. The long course pool is four feet at both ends, seven feet in the middle, and an average depth of six feet.

The DKAC also features newly renovated locker rooms and showers for both the men's and women's swimming & diving teams and women's water polo squad. The improvements, which also included a new conference room, were completed in the summer of 2011. 

The competition pool doubles as the diving facility, which has two one-meter and three-meter springboards. The diving/competition pool features three platform towers at heights of five, seven-and-a-half, and 10-meters. It also houses a hot tub and an underwater viewing room where the swimmers are videotaped, enabling the coaching staff to analyze each swimmer’s stroke mechanics. This is one of the fastest pools in the USA at a depth of 17 feet.

The DKAC was constructed in stages with the pool opening in 1978. Other facets of the complex were added later, with construction culminating in 1986. In the summer of 2001, major renovations were made to the diving facilities. Concrete bases were created for the springboards and new platform surfaces and stairs were added. These improvements along with a new warm-up area, provide the UH divers an excellent facility for training and competition. New 6-inch short course lane lines and a top-of-the-line Paragon starting blocks have made swimming at the “Duke” better and faster than ever.

The pool stands as a namesake to Hawai‘i’s most legendary swimmer and surfer, Duke Kahanamoku. “The Duke” was the first famous Waikiki beach boy. His passion for water sports included surfing, swimming and canoeing, taking him to the 1912 Olympics in Stockholm, Sweden. Kahanamoku shocked the world when he broke the 100-yard freestyle event and received his gold medal by an impressed Swede King Gustaf.

At age 34, Duke brought home a silver medal in the 100-meter at the 1924 Olympiad in Paris. He attended the Olympics again in 1932 at the age of 42 with the U.S. water polo team. His death on Jan. 22, 1968, left Hawai‘i missing a hero.

clubs.title: Swim Lessons Hawaii Masters Swimming
clubs.description:
Swim Lessons Hawaii Masters Swimming (SLHMS)

Location: Pearl City, Hawai‘i (Manana Housing USMC)  
Membership: Open exclusively to military personnel and their families  

Swim Lessons Hawaii Masters Swimming (SLHMS) is Hawai‘i’s premier swim program, offering elite-level training at a brand-new facility designed specifically for military members. Our program provides structured workouts for all skill levels, from beginners to competitive swimmers, helping each athlete build endurance, improve technique, and enhance overall performance.  

Why Train with SLHMS?
✔ State-of-the-art training pool 
✔ 7-day-a-week training – morning & evening sessions available  
✔ Three on-deck coaches  at all times for personalized guidance  
✔ Certified USMS Adult Learn-to-Swim (ALTS) instructors 
✔ Certified lifeguards  ensuring maximum safety at every practice  
✔ Invitation-only access for military members and their families  
✔ Exclusive parking area  for club members  

Programs Offered: 
🏊 Masters Swimming Club Training – Scalable workouts tailored for all levels  
🏊 Adult Learn to Swim (ALTS) – USMS-certified instruction for beginners  
🏊 Lap Swim Preparation – Technique and endurance-focused training  
🏊 Open Water Championship & Channel Swimming Prep – Specialized training for distance swimming, navigation, and race strategy

SLHMS is a USMS Gold Club, ensuring the highest level of coaching and community support. Whether you're training for open water events, preparing for military fitness tests, or just looking to stay active in the water, SLHMS provides an unparalleled swimming experience.  

📩  Interested in joining?  Visit our website or contact us at hello@swimlessonshawaii.co

clubs.description: Southern Oregon Masters Aquatics is a group of swimmers, varying in age from 18 to over 80, with a wide variety of interests and reasons for swimming.  Most of us live in the Medford, Oregon area but we are scattered throughout the sparsely-populated area of southern Oregon and northern California.  While the majority of us swim at Superior Athletic Club in Medford, there are two other SOMA groups of notable mention; one in Klamath Falls swimming at the Ella Redkey Pool and another in Coos Bay swimming at the Mingus Park Pool.  The culture of SOMA is very fun, kind, welcoming, motivated, hard-working, positive and supportive of all things aquatic and each other.

clubs.title: Columbia Gorge Masters
