clubs.title: Rogue Valley Masters
clubs.description: Founded in Ashland in 1984, RVM has all levels of swimmers, including those who like to compete in pool meets, open water events, and multi-sport events, as well as those who primarily swim for fitness. Our common ground is a love for swimming, and we take turns coaching each other from the deck with 6 workouts per week. We welcome all swimmers who are able to swim at least a 50 freestyle without stopping. We work out in the pool year-round and in the local lakes in the summer open water season. We love having new folks join us, as well as those traveling through or visiting Ashland!
clubs.practiceTimes: We swim at Daniel Meyer Pool (outdoors) in Hunter Park, in Ashland, Monday - Friday 5:45-6:45 AM, Sunday 7:45-8:45 AM. Tuesday and Thursday have extended hours until 8:00 AM. We arrive 15 minutes early to uncover the pool and put the lane lines in. Enter the pool from the gate on the parking lot side; the pool is not open to the general public when we swim. We take turns coaching ourselves on a rotating basis.

clubs.title: Richmond Plunge Masters
clubs.description: Richmond Plunge Masters is a club of about 70 swimmers with 9 coached workouts a week. We are based at the Richmond Plunge, an historic indoor swimming facility built in 1926 and restored in 2010.  The Plunge is a 5-minute walk to Keller Beach, a sandy cove on the east shore San Francisco Bay, where many of us swim open water year-round, and where we host an annual open water swim event - Keller Cove Swim for Kids' Sake.  Our club is also known for our Swimming Saves Lives adult learn to swim program, helping to bring basic swimming and water safety instruction to a large population of at risk adults in our community.

clubs.title: Gold's Aquatics Club Camas
clubs.description: The Gold’s Aquatics Club Camas (GACC) Masters is the USA swimming Masters training group out of the Gold’s Gym Aquatic Center serving individuals who are 18 years and older. We are an inclusive community that serves individuals newer to swimming as well as those who swam competitively in the past. Our coaches create an inclusive, supportive, and encouraging environment in which our Masters group thrives within. Our coaches deliver challenging practices that engage the body and mind while address technique and the individual needs of our swimmers.
clubs.practiceTimes: GACC Masters have 5-6 one-hour long practices per week in the pool and have access to all the amenities of our gym, including our personal training group fitness class. Our practices are dynamic and follow a non-traditional training cycle that allows each individual to gain benefits from training even if they are unable to practice each day, unlike a traditional training cycle.

clubs.title: Alameda Aquatic Masters
clubs.description:
Our main goal at Alameda Aquatic Masters is to provide the team with a fun, active, and successful community where you can accomplish your swimming goals, build relationships, and (my favorite) have fun! We all know that swimming can be a monotonous sport, but with the right mix of workouts, activities, and social interactions, the pool is truly one of the best places to be. We aim to give you a workout that is suited for your level, whatever that may be, and will work with each of you on goals you may have - whether that is to stay fit, train for an open water swim/triathlon/swim meet, or even just to become a better lap swimmer!
.

clubs.title: Vacaville Swim Club
clubs.description:
Our Masters program is a blend of fitness, competitive, and triathlete swimming for adults (18 years of age and over). The regular structured workouts use techniques of interval training in an atmosphere of fun, fitness, friendship and participation! Individuals who find it difficult to stick with a self-imposed, keep-fit routine will find it easier to maintain a Masters Swimming program. We welcome swimmers of all abilities. Whether you want to improve your overall fitness, swim for fun, develop better technique, or train for triathlons, open water swims, or pool swimming competitions, our workouts are designed to challenge people of all abilities.

We have an amazing staff of outstanding, experienced coaches who are on deck for our swimmers 6 days a week!

clubs.title: East Bay Bat Rays Masters
clubs.description:
The East Bay Bat Rays Masters (EBAT) provides organized workouts, competitions, clinics and workshops for adults 18 and over. The program is perfect for all adults - fitness swimmers, triathletes, college students, competitors, and beginners - who are interested in improving their fitness and their lives through swimming.

Each workout is led by a highly experienced coach (see coach bios on main EBAT website) who will provide technical feedback, organize the training session, and help members achieve their swimming goals. And perhaps most importantly, Masters Swimming is a great milieu for a healthy lifestyle through friendship and camaraderie.

clubs.title: Tsunami Breakers Open Ocean Masters
clubs.description: TBOOM swims both in the pool and in the waters of Monterey Bay. We welcome fitness swimmers through advanced-competitive athletes who wish to build upon their existing competencies. When in the pool, coached workouts feature interval training to develop strength, power, technique and stamina with added focus on form, breath control, starts and turns. While in the open water, coached workouts feature pacing, forward sighting, rounding buoys and identifying and utilizing currents to best advantage. Regardless of your starting skill level, swimming promotes cardio-vascular endurance, enhanced balance, core stabilization, body coordination, and TBOOM swimmers enjoy camaraderie and a sense of adventure.
clubs.practiceTimes: TBOOM is currently swimming: Monday / Wednesday / Friday 5:30PM to 7:00PM at the Pacific Grove High School Pool and by announcement in the open water.

clubs.description: Most team members belong to Broadstone Sports Club located in Folsom, CA. If you are looking to swim workout with us, then you will need to become a member or the sports club. Our favorite place is lane 9 (hot tub) at the end of a hard workout!
clubs.practiceTimes: Monday, Wednesday, Friday is a coached practice from 6 AM to 7 AM. Tuesday, Thursday is an unofficial non coached group that swims from 6 AM to 7 AM. On Saturdays, there is an unofficial non coached group that swims from 7:30 AM to 8:30 AM. The groups during the unofficial time may be anywhere from 0-10 swimmers. It all just depends on work/life schedules. In the summer, we may venture to open water if we are brave enough! Some of our other clubs also have Masters swim practices. Let me know if you have questions about any other Sparetime locations.

clubs.title: Clovis Swim Club Masters
clubs.description:
Clovis Swim Club Masters provides organized workouts, competitions, clinics and workshops for adults 18 and over. Programs are open to all adult swimmers fitness, triathlete, competitive, non-competitive, who are dedicated to improving their fitness through swimming.

     Structured workouts offer well thought-out training assistance. Pool and open-water races provide opportunities to compete and measure individual progress at the local, state, national and international levels.  USMS programs also offer stroke and technique clinics, workshops and instructions, as well as social functions.

     Sign up online! 

   Clovis Swim Club Masters Swimming Fee Schedule:                  

Each new account is charged the once annual Administrative Fee of $100.00 covering your administrative obligations from August - July.
Any competitive members must self-register with United States Masters Swimming to be eligible for competition.
Program Dues:
Ø      Masters Members: can attend any Masters practices and have three payment options:
1)     $50.00/ month –or-
2)     $270.00 semi annual (Aug- Jan) and (Feb.- July). That’s a 10% discount -or-
3) $510.00 annual payment option (Aug- July), that's a 15% discount.
Please note: Payments made monthly must be on auto-pay through our club website.
 

Pacific Masters

clubs.title: Flathead Lake Open Water Swimmers
clubs.description: FLOW Swimmers, or Flathead Lake Open Water Swimmers, is a community-based organization located in Polson, MT, on the south shore of Flathead Lake. The group is passionate about both pool and open water swimming. Members range from beginners to seasoned athletes, and the organization offers a variety of training sessions in both environments. FLOW Swimmers also hosts events and open water challenges to keep everyone engaged and foster a sense of community. It's a fantastic way for people to connect, improve their swimming skills, and enjoy the natural beauty of Flathead Lake as well as the discipline of pool swimming.  We have swimmers from Missoula to Whitefish and everywhere in between.

Within the program, structured workouts from the coaches offer valuable training assistance along with stroke and technique instruction. This program is designed to help swimmers improve fitness and/or train for specific goals, and offer active support for a healthy lifestyle through friendship and camaraderie.

clubs.title: Southern California Aquatic Masters
clubs.description: Bring us your swim and fitness goals!  SCAQ is the largest Masters swimming program in the United States and one of the oldest established in 1979. We are a swimming club with a lane for every level of adult swimmer, beginner, novice and Olympian offering over 80 weekly workouts in ten of the best pools around LA County and an eleventh in the Pacific Ocean.   You will find an experienced coached organized workout morning, noon and night seven days a week along with clinics, private instruction and participation in local, regional, and national competitions: pool and ocean.  You are welcome to try a swim!  And get a video you.

clubs.title: Imagine Swimming Masters
clubs.description: Imagine Swimming Masters is a natural extension of Imagine Swimming, Inc. -- New York City's premier aquatics program since 2002. While our Masters program is registered & based in Los Angeles (where one of the two Imagine founders now resides), the club welcomes remote members from anywhere & everywhere, and seeks to build an extended community of former club & college swimmers who've returned to the sport as Masters.
clubs.practiceTimes: The club's 'base' is the Culver City Plunge on the westside of LA. However, practices are informal and may be conducted in any pool available to you, with workouts shared online. Members are encouraged to share sets, season plans, etc. with a communal, all-are-welcome approach.

clubs.title: West Hollywood Aquatics
clubs.description:
WH2O is home to over 300 athletes from Southern California, ranging from ex-Olympians to beginners. We believe that everyone is welcome, regardless of their skill level, gender, race, sexual orientation, or disability. At WH2O, we believe that diversity is our strength. 

WH2O offers professionally coached daily workouts and seasonal clinics in 3 aquatics disciplines: Swimming, Water Polo, Ocean. 

Our members across all 3 disciplines compete in events, meets, and tournaments at the Regional, National, and International levels.

clubs.title: Lion Aquatic Masters
clubs.description:
Lion Aquatic Masters is a year-round adult aquatic swimming program for 
all levels and abilities from ‘newbie’ novice to competitive swimmers and triathletes. We offer 15 weekly workouts to fit your busy schedule.   Our experienced coaches design challenging workouts tailored to your skill level while providing stroke instruction and training tips to help you improve technique and maximize cardiovascular output.  While the emphasis is on fitness, the atmosphere is a general mix of dedication and fun.
clubs.practiceTimes: Pool is 25 yards x 50 meters.  Long course training on Thurs/Sat mornings. For more detail see website: www.lmumasters.com

clubs.title: Redondo Dolphin Club Swim Team
clubs.description:
We are an open water ONLY swim team and do not have access to a pool. We swim in the Pacific Ocean. New and trial members joining us for a workout should be experienced open water swimmers, or pool swimmers who can swim at least 1000 yards without stopping, and are not afraid of the ocean and are interested in learning about ocean swimming.  We have swimmers of every pace and you do not need to be fast to join our team. We swim all year round. Some of our members wear wetsuits and others swim "skins" without wetsuits even in winter. We are based in Redondo Beach and also swim at nearby beaches. Many of our members are also triathletes. When they are offered, we also compete in local swim meets.
 Our team no longer has access to a pool but many of us swim laps at various community pools, in addition to our ocean swims.
clubs.practiceTimes: Ocean workouts vary weekly and are for registered members only, and those on official  trial memberships. Our practices generally begin between the hours of 9:00AM- 5:30PM, Tuesday -Saturday. Most active members of our team are either self employed, students, teachers, retired, or have flexible schedules. We usually offer 1 ocean swim workout per week on weekends.

clubs.title: Burbank Aquatics Masters
clubs.description: Burbank Aquatics Masters (BAM) is a nonprofit adult aquatics organisation dedicated to promoting lifelong fitness, health, and water safety through structured swim training, Adult Learn to Swim (ALTS) instruction, education, and community engagement. Operating as a registered U.S. Masters Swimming club, BAM offers coached swim practices, technique clinics, ALTS programmes for adults of all experience levels, and opportunities to participate in sanctioned competitions and community swim events. All programmes are welcoming, inclusive, and designed to help adults build confidence, improve technique, and stay safe in and around the water.
clubs.practiceTimes: Practices will commence in January 2026, with coached sessions offered three days per week. Each practice will run approximately 1 to 1.5 hours. Additional practice times and programme offerings will be added as the club grows and community demand increases.

clubs.title: Golden Road Aquatics
clubs.description: Golden Road Aquatics (GRA) is a 501(c)(3) organization. GRA has grown from a small fitness and competitive team into a highly-rated organization. Our mission is to empower all swimmers and fitness levels to become their own champion in life. Our model of excellence became instant success and is embraced by others in the swimming community and around the globe. Our unique program is comprised of 150 swimmers, triathletes and professional swimmers pursuing international competition and the Olympic dream. Golden Road Aquatics is a great way to keep fit, make friends and have fun, while being a part of a healthy activity that can be continued for a lifetime.

clubs.title: Sierra Nevada Masters at Incline Village
clubs.description: The Incline Village branch of Sierra Nevada Masters encourages swimmers of all levels of ability and interest to join us. We support swimmers interested in competition up to the U. S. national level, those interested in triathlon and those interested in fitness. Workouts are printed and distributed at the beginning of each workout. We do not have a coach on deck at this time but will consider hiring a coach if our numbers grow sufficiently. Workout are geared to the ability of the swimmer.
clubs.practiceTimes: We sponsor 4 workout a week at the Inclline Village Recreation Center. Use of the pool requires membership in the Rec. Center or a drop-in fee the amounts of which are determined by whether or not you are a resident of Incline Village. Workout times: Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday and Saturday. Weekdays are from 6:00 am to about 7:15. am and Saturday is from 7:00 am to about 8:15 am. During the summer months, we often workout in Lake Tahoe starting at about 8:00 am on Saturday morning.

clubs.title: Frog Aquatics Club
clubs.description:
Frog Aquatics Club is a year-round, swim team and lesson provider serving the Antelope Valley and surrounding areas.

At Frog Aquatics Club, we are united by our love for swimming and a shared commitment to excellence. Our mission is to create an environment that is inclusive and welcoming to all, where every swimmer and student feels valued and supported. We believe in the importance of building strong relationships, both in and out of the pool. Through teamwork, respect, and camaraderie, we aim to create a close-knit family atmosphere that extends beyond competitive swimming.

Our focus goes beyond producing strong athletes; we are dedicated to developing individuals of character. We strive to instill values of perseverance, integrity, and discipline in our swimmers and students, preparing them for success both in the water and in life.

At the heart of our mission is a passion for swimming. We aim to instill a lifelong love for the sport in each of our members, fostering a joy and enthusiasm that transcends competition.

At Frogs, we are committed to nurturing the growth of the whole person, celebrating diversity, and empowering our members to reach their fullest potential, both as athletes and as individuals
clubs.practiceTimes: Practices are 4:30-6:30pm Monday-Friday, at Courson Park Pool.

clubs.title: Rose Bowl Masters
clubs.description:
The Rose Bowl Masters Swim Team is a team-based adult training program. Coach Chad Durieux supervises the morning, afternoon, and evening workouts. Swimming skill-levels range from novice competitive swimmers to former Olympic competitors. Lanes are organized with swimmers grouped according to ability. Some members are training for specific events while most train for broad health and fitness reasons. The atmosphere is a general mixture of dedication and fun.
Visitor drop-ins are welcome during any of our workout times but registration is on hold for new members because we are at capacity. 
 Please Contact Coach Chad if you have registration questions or would like to drop in for a visit.

clubs.title: Bainbridge Aquatic Masters
clubs.description:
BAM is a vibrant group of adults who enjoy Masters Swimming for the camaraderie, the competitions, the fitness opportunities, and the ability to make a difference in our community through swimming.  We have a wide range of abilities and welcome everyone where they are; from our learn to swim athletes to our Olympians!  Our coaches help with goal setting, technique and training in pool, open water and dryland practices. 
Can't wait to have you join us!
clubs.practiceTimes:
Practices are open to all levels and abilities and are held in the Ray Pool at the Bainbridge Aquatic Center
Due to team size and lane availability, athletes are encouraged to pre-register to attend and claim a lane space at practice.  Please contact Coach April Cheadle at AprilC@biparks.org for the Sign-up link to pre-register.  We are currently offering between 4-6 daily practices Monday-Saturday mornings through lunch hour.

clubs.title: SWIM OPEN WATERS
clubs.description:
Open water swimming enthusiasts welcome.
A safe, supportive place to learn open-water skills, build confidence, improve efficiency, and seek adventure. Open to swimmers of all levels.
clubs.practiceTimes:
Monday Mornings | 7:00–8:00am
Ventura Cove

A co-coached, circuit-based open-water swim in a calm bay setting. New swimmers should check in with the coaches before swimming for a brief safety talk.

Swimmers meet at Ventura Cove and swim counterclockwise around five buoys. Practices focus on technique, efficiency, speed, drafting, and building comfort and confidence in open water. Designed for all levels of swimmers.

We also regularly host adventure swims and open-water form clinics.

clubs.title: San Diego Swim Masters
clubs.description: San Diego Swim Masters (SDSM) was established in 1971 and was known as San Diego County Swim Masters (SDCSM) until 1980 when the name was shortened to San Diego Swim Masters (SDSM).  SDSM swimmers train in various pools across San Diego County under a variety of coaches, on their own, or elsewhere.  Workout/practice times and fees vary by facility.  SDSM members have the opportunity to swim on relays with the assistance of the SDSM relay coordinator.  SDSM's permanent club ID in SI LMSC is #001.
clubs.practiceTimes:
Workout/practice times and fees vary depending upon the facility/pool location, workout group, and pool status.  Please directly contact individual facilities for the most up-to-date information about workout times and fees, visitor drop-in fees, and joining the workout group.
    The Masters workout group at UCSD (Canyonview Aquatic Complex, University of California at San Diego, 3390 Voigt Drive, La Jolla, CA 92093) has several practice options, 6-7:30 am M-F, 6-7:30 pm M-Th, and 7:30-9 am Sat.  Please see the UCSD Recreation website (https://recreation.ucsd.edu/competitive-sports/masters/) for fees and information.  For current drop-in or visitor rates, please click on the “Pricing” drop down menu.  For the workout schedule, please click on the “Schedule” drop down menu.  Note a parking permit is required at all times on campus OR park at metered or “parking app” spaces.  Please contact UCSD Recreation for information about signing up for workout times and fees.
     Masters workouts at the Mission Valley Y, located at 5505 Friars Rd., San Diego, CA 92110, (https://www.ymcasd.org/locations/mission-valley-ymca), are for Mission Valley Y members, participants, and drop-ins.  The Masters swim program is a structured swim workout designed to help avid recreational and competitive swimmers of various skill levels to improve stroke technique, form, ability, endurance and stamina. Meet like-minded swimmers and make new friends.  All practices are held in the 10-lane outdoor Mission Valley Y Bruce Hazard pool.  Register online, via telephone, or in person at the Mission Valley Y branch.  For inquiries, updates, and information, please call the front desk at 619-298-3576 or contact the aquatics director Jordan Van Norman, jvannorman@ymcasd.org, 619-298-3576 ext. 2067.  Subject to change, the monthly masters practice times are: M-F 6–7 am, 7–8 am, and 12-1 pm; and Sat 8–9:30 am.  Daily fees are $10 per Y member or $25 per day for non-members.  Click on “Pool Schedule” at the bottom of the MVY home page at https://www.ymcasd.org/locations/mission-valley-ymca.  Please contact the Mission Valley Y for information about signing up for workout times and fees.
      Other Masters clubs and workout groups exist in the San Diego and Imperial Counties geographical area covered by the San Diego-Imperial Local Masters Swimming Committee (SI LMSC, website   www.SIMasterswim.org).  Please check the list of pools, workout locations, lap swim locations, and club information for San Diego-Imperial Local Masters Swimming Committee (SI LMSC) at http://www.simasterswim.org/WorkOutLocations.pdf.  
Please call the individual facility for current information.

We aim to foster fun, well-being, health, and positive experiences through sport, competition, and social events as a member club of United States Masters Swimming.  DSST welcomes people of all sexual preferences and identities, although special outreach and support to LGBTQIA+ communities continues to be an essential element of our mission. clubs.title: Shadow Hills Masters Swimming
clubs.description:
The Shadow Hills Masters
provides adults 18 years of age and older the opportunity to swim in an organized program aimed at meeting swimmers’ individual goals. The program allows swimmers to challenge and improve their swimming ability while engaging with others in friendly learning environment. 

Shadow Hills Masters offers clear instruction and professional training motivating swimmers to be their best, build camaraderie and engage in friendly competition.  Shadow Hills Masters is open to everyone regardless of swimming ability or experience. 

Shadow Hills Masters is the only US Masters Swim Club in the Coachella Valley with USA Swimming Certified swim coaches. The professional coaches of Shadow Hills Masters provide swimmers with the opportunity to focus their efforts in such areas as general fitness / wellness, competition, building endurance and stroke improvement. Those seeking an environment to train for a triathlon or open water swim would also find themselves at home in this program. Our professional coaches run structured workouts and activities at various levels based on individual abilities and what each person seeks from being a masters swimmer.

Coaches provide knowledge, guidance, direction, encouragement and motivation on an individualized basis. Most important, our coaches ensure swimmers feel welcome and have fun while staying motivated to continue their involvement in this lifelong sport.

clubs.title: Bozeman Masters Swimming Club
clubs.description: The Bozeman Masters Swim Club has been an official U.S. Masters Swimming club since 2004. The volunteer-run, nonprofit program welcomes adult swimmers of all ages, abilities, and motivations. The club’s membership represents a diverse cross-section of the Bozeman community. The club has a strong team of 7 coaches who cater to the diverse needs of the membership. We offer structured, full-body workouts to enhance fitness, core strength, aerobic conditioning, physical endurance, and stroke technique. For more information, visit http://www.bozemanmasters.org/.
clubs.practiceTimes: Changes seasonally. Please see our website (bozemanmasters.org) for current practice schedules.

clubs.title: Patriot Swim Club
clubs.description:
Patriot Swim Club is a year-round competitive swim program based in Irvine, California, dedicated to helping athletes of all ages and abilities reach their fullest potential — both in and out of the water. Our mission is to build strong, confident, and disciplined swimmers through expert coaching, teamwork, and a positive, supportive training environment.
We operate at two locations: Northpark Community Pool and Beckman High School Pool.
Our programs range from learn-to-swim and pre-competitive groups to adult learn-to-swim and masters training, ensuring that every swimmer can progress at their own pace and comfort level.
Our experienced coaching staff emphasizes proper technique, endurance, and a lifelong love for the sport, while instilling the core values of respect, perseverance, and sportsmanship.
Patriot Swim Club is a proud member of USA Swimming, and our athletes regularly participate in local and regional competitions, representing the club with pride and passion.
clubs.practiceTimes:
We currently offer our Masters practices three times per week — on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday from 7:00 to 8:00 PM.
Practices are held at both of our locations: Northpark Community Pool and Beckman High School Pool.

clubs.title: SADDLEBROOKE MASTERS
clubs.description:
SaddleBrooke Masters is hosted by the SaddleBrooke Swim Club, an activity of SaddleBrooke—an active adult community for residents age 55+. While most of our swimmers live in SaddleBrooke, you do not need to live in SaddleBrooke or be over 55 to compete with our team at swim meets.
Compete With Us
•	Non-residents welcome at meets
•	No age requirement
•	Must be a current USMS (U.S. Masters Swimming) member
We’re known for being fun, relaxed, and well organized, with excellent, experienced coaches who attend meets and actively support all SaddleBrooke Masters swimmers—residents and non-residents alike. Whether it’s your first meet or your fiftieth, you’ll have help with entries, warm-ups, race strategy, and encouragement on deck.
Note: Swim practices are limited to SaddleBrooke residents only.
________________________________________
Supporting Programs: Building Strong Swimmers
In addition to Masters swimming, the SaddleBrooke Swim Club offers programs that support our resident swimmers at every level:
Let’s Swim Clinic (Residents Only)
A technique- and endurance-focused clinic for adults who want to lap swim more confidently and efficiently. Small groups and expert coaching make this a great bridge into regular swimming.
•	Offered twice yearly
•	FREE of charge
Adult Learn to Swim (Residents Only)
Designed for adults with little or no swimming experience, or those uncomfortable in the water. This clinic focuses on water safety, comfort, and basic swimming skills in a calm, supportive environment.
•	Offered twice yearly
•	FREE of charge
Both programs are led by our outstanding coaches and reflect the club’s commitment to supporting swimming in our community.
📧 For schedules, details, or general questions:
Email us at saddlebrookeswimclub.org

clubs.title: Casper WY Knots
clubs.description: Wyoming's first Masters Swim Club, based in Casper, WY. We practice at the YMCA 5 days a week and welcome adult swimmers aged 19 and over, of all levels! Our group loves welcoming new swimmers just starting out or seasoned lovers of the sport. Coach Dean Hawks has 52 years of competitive swimming experience, teaching everyone from beginners to world champions. Coach Hawks was awarded the National Masters Coach of the Year in 2020 and was part of a team that took Masters Swimmers to the world championship in Montreal, Canada! The Casper WY Knots have created a great community of swimmers and look forward to expanding!

clubs.title: Loveland Masters Swimming
clubs.description:
We welcome beginner swimmers to elite competitors and everyone in between. We have something to offer adults of every age and skill level. If you want to get fit, become a better swimmer, stay motivated, and meet new friends, Loveland Masters is the place to be. We offer coaching for:
Fitness
Technique
Open Water
Triathlon
Our members come from a variety of swimming backgrounds, from Olympic medalists to people who picked up the sport later in life. Our swimmers have a wide variety of interests beyond swimming, including hikers, road and mountain bikers, triathletes, runners, yoga enthusiasts, and skiers. Our members come from a wide variety of professions, from accountants and doctors to landscape engineers and marketing professionals. If you like to exercise in the morning, you'll fit in with our group!

Our coaches will challenge you with structured workouts, designed to improve your endurance, strength, speed and power in the water. clubs.title: Roadrunners of Albuquerque Aquatic Club
clubs.description:
The Roadrunners of Albuquerque Aquatic Club Masters (RAACM) is a group of people who enjoy swimming for its health, recreational and physical fitness benefits. We are comprised of all age groups (18yrs+) and ability levels, from swimming novices to Nationally recognized competitors. We also offer great swim instruction & training for all Triathletes in the Metro area. 

As a Roadrunners Master swimmer, you will have the option to participate in coached workouts, lap swimming, and Masters swim competitions.  We also offer private swim instruction for those seeking one-on-one time for stroke/endurance improvement.

The first week will be a free trial week. Then starting on 9/16, if you decide to join, your month of sept will be prorated. The group swims M-F 530-630a at West Mesa Pool. The cost will be $99 a month.

clubs.title: Wright County Wave Swim Club
clubs.description:
CLUB MISSION
The Wright County Wave Swim Club is a United States Swimming competitive team dedicated to providing a safe and supportive environment where successful athletes are developed. A successful athlete is one empowered by not only the physical fitness benefits associated with swimming but also with the lifelong skills of determination, discipline and integrity.

CLUB HISTORY
Competitive swimming was first introduced in Buffalo in the 1980's by BHM Community Education as a winter program. Immediately after that program ended, Buffalo swimmers and parents sought out the nearest USA Swimming program so that their athletes could continue to train and compete in the spring and summer. For years, every spring, Buffalo swimmers would travel to Monticello (the nearest USA Swim Club at the time) every day so that they could continue to train and attend meets. As interest in swimming as a sport grew larger in Buffalo parents decided to create their own local, year-round USA Swimming program and the Wavemakers Bison Swim Association was formed. This organization not only ran the Buffalo USA Swimming Club, they also were the booster club for the Bison Girl's Swim and Diving Team. In 1993, the parent board changed the name of the club to the Wright County Wave Swim Club. This name change was made due to the increasing number of swimmers from other local cities near Buffalo not associated with the Bison.

THE CLUB TODAY
Today, the Wright County Wave Swim Club is still a non-profit USA Swimming Club, run by a member elected Board of Directors. The Board of Directors are volunteers. Their purpose as a board is to support local competitive swimmers by overseeing the operations of the club. The Board meets monthly and all Wave Swim Club parents and adult members are welcome to attend.

The Board believes in swimming as a life long sport so all swimmers ages 6 and up who live in or near Wright County are encouraged to join. The club welcomes swimmers at every level of ability, from novice to international competitor. A current USA swimming membership or a current U.S. Masters Swimming Membership is required to swim practice with Wave. Wave promotes the sport of swimming; so swimmers are required to participate in swim meets. Home swim meet attendance is required. All swimmers will be encouraged to attend the Minnesota Swimming Championship Meets at the end of each season.  

The club runs two sessions each year that coincide with the Minnesota Swimming Long Course Season and Minnesota Swimming Short Course Season. The Long Course Season begins in April and runs into the end of July. The Short Course Season begins in September and runs into the end of February/early March of the following year. Registration will open on-line on the Wave Swim Club website approximately one month prior to the beginning of each season. The club requires all swimmers and coaches to become USA Swimming members. A discounted USA Swimming Seasonal Membership is offered for swimmers who join in April and only swim the Long Course Season.

Practices take place at the Buffalo Community Middle School Pool. Practices during the short course season will be held using the 25 yard portion of the pool. Long Course Season practices will make use of the 25 meter portion of the pool.

CLUB VISION 
The Wright County Wave Swim Club views competitive swimming and all sports as a means to learn valuable life skills. Swim meets and competition are simply a way to realize one’s own abilities and growth. The final measure is not if you placed first or how much time you dropped, but what you learned during the competitive experience. Wave Swim Club.

Whether you're a first-time swimmer, a seasoned athlete, a young adult, a triathlete, or a fitness enthusiast, our inclusive group has a place for you. Affiliated with both Minnesota Masters and United States Masters Swimming, a national organization championing adult health, wellness, fitness, and competitive swimming, Foxjets Masters offers a unique blend of camaraderie, coaching, and a supportive team environment.

​

Guided by a qualified swim coach, our program tailors a variety of workouts to accommodate different fitness levels, skills, and experiences. The coach focuses on enhancing swimming techniques and improving overall fitness. clubs.title: Riptide Masters
clubs.description:
Riptide Masters and Tri-Athlete Training Program (Ages 18 and older)
The Riptide Team is committed to developing lifelong skills and healthy living.  In keeping with this commitment, Riptide's Masters and Tri-athlete Training programs will provide 18+ year olds a vehicle to meet the individual needs of Master's Swimmers and Tri-athletes.  Whether it is a desire to compete at a higher level, train for the swim leg of a triathlon or exercise to stay fit,  we will offer workouts, coaching and support customized to reach those goals. Our staff is always on deck and work just as hard at your success as you do. Our Masters-Tri Program is coached by Amy Parratto and Peggy Kratz. The cost is currently $60.00 per month.
clubs.practiceTimes:
Riptide Masters practices Monday through Saturday from 5:30 to 6:45 a.m. We also offer 7:15 to 8:30 a.m. practices on Tuesday and Thursday's. Bathrooms are available, but no swimmers may use the locker rooms to shower or change on T/TH/S's because of dual use with our age group program. Showers can be used after our M/W/F practices.
Our Masters practices are designed in a similar manner to our age group practices. We all swim practices in a "team" format. That means that all swimmers will start the practice at the same time! The workouts will have two to three different levels with either different distances or intervals. If you need to leave early for work...no problem! Our goal is to create more of a "team" atmosphere which then adds to the level of motivation and quality of the practice. Our coaches are committed to helping adult swimmers achieve their athletic and fitness goals. Additionally, Masters swimmers are encouraged to ask the coaches for help with their technique, training and competition goals. We hope you join us!!!!

clubs.title: Swim Free or Die
clubs.description:
We are offering a Masters swim group at Evergreen Sports Club in Lisbon, NH, for swimmers aged eighteen and over.
Don’t let the name scare you!
Masters swimming is open to any adult of any age who has a passion for swimming. You don’t have to be fast, you don’t have to know all the strokes, you don’t have to look good in a bathing suit, you just have to want to swim. If you want to improve your swimming, if you want to practice for a triathlon, or you just want to hang out with other people who get excited about new goggles, join us! Practices are tailored to each swimmer’s ability. This is free to Evergreen pool members, and $15 a session without a membership. A punch card is available with 10 sessions for $120.
For more information, email us at evergreenswimmers@gmail.com. For more information
on United States Masters Swimming (USMS) non profit organization, go to www.usms.org. You do not have to be a USMS member to swim with us.
clubs.practiceTimes:

clubs.title: Slow 2 Fast Swimming, Inc.
clubs.description: Slow 2 Fast Swimming, Inc (S2F) offers 1:1 online video instruction for individualized technique and personalized skill work as well as in person clinics at the pool(s) you are currently training in.  Simple and Effective; we come to you.  Scheduled by Appointment.
clubs.practiceTimes:
Your pool.  Your stroke.  Your focus.  Your improvement. Our support.
Slow down, receive customized instruction, apply it, and get faster: Slow2Fast Swimming, Inc. 

2 Ways to Work with us!
Slow 2 Fast Swimming, Inc works 1:1 with swimmers via an online platform.  You film you, we review with you, and you receive a recording of your specific instruction including personalized drill and technique work for your stroke right now.  S2F Swimming, Inc also offers in person clinics and travels to your pool/town/area. Clinics run about 2 hours and some clinics may be customizable to provide underwater videos and analysis.  Swimmers or coaches who assist in organizing the in-person clinics may participate in the clinic for free.  Contact Tara Mack, aka “TMack'' for more details.  
https://slow2fastswimming.org
All swimmers must be U.S. Masters Swimming Members, ask me about how to join. We offer a 30 Day Free Trial for USMS, available upon request and also available in person at clinics.

clubs.title: Omaha Masters Swim Club
clubs.description: If you're looking to get a good workout, make some friends, and improve your swimming while you're at it, then the Omaha Masters is right for you! Masters swimming is not necessarily about competitions, racing, or getting ready for swim meets. It can be, but that depends on the goals and aspirations of each individual. Omaha Masters recognizes this and works with individual swimmers to achieve their desired goals. For some, it might be about getting a good low-impact workout. Others are working to improve their swimming for triathlon competitions. All of us, in one way or another, are gently guided to improve each aspect of our swimming--our stroke, turns, endurance, etc.

labels.noClubsText:
Didn't find a club in your area?

Leave us your information and we'll notify you when a club opens near you.
labels.noClubsTrialText:
Didn't find a club in your area?

Expand your range on this form or view all of our clubs in the Club Finder. Or, you can leave us your information and we'll notify you when a club opens near you.
labels.clubFinderNoClubsWelcomeText: Thank you! Your information will help us find a way to start a new club in your area. While we work on that, you can still join USMS and take advantage of our other benefits, like our Workout Library, in-person and virtual events, exclusive content, partner discounts, and online community.
labels.trialMembershipNoClubsWelcomeText: Thank you! Your information will help us find a way to start a new program in your area. While we work on that, you can view all of our clubs using the Club Finder tool or review some of our other benefits that can help you find workouts and a community virtually.
